items.title: [New Atlas] X camper van steers space-shifting wizardry deep off-road
items.content_html:
🎨 🌾 🌲 ✏️

If you ask us, off-road camping is the best kind of camping. But hours after hours of brutal, hard-hammering, slow-crawling trail driving can also leave the body aching before you even step out of the vehicle. Unfortunately, many of the best 4x4 camper vans will require you to squeeze those aching limbs into an undersized bed at night. But the new Bürstner Habiton X gives you an agile Sprinter 144 on the trail and your own personal longitudinal bed at camp. That's all thanks to its space-optimizing transformer capabilities.

Continue Reading

Category: Campervans, Adventure Vehicles, Outdoors

Tags: burstner, Campervan, Off-road, Off-grid, Hymer, Transformable, RV, Outdoors and Camping, Camping

items.title: [New Atlas] Robotic elephant-trunk gripper uses internal camera for a sense of touch
items.content_html:
🎨 🌾 🔧 🪶 🌲 🏔️ 🔭 ✏️

🎨 🌾 🔧 🪶 🌲 🏔️ 🔭 ✏️

Evolution has already found solutions to lots of problems, so engineers more often borrow ideas from nature instead of reinventing the wheel. We’ve previously seen robots that can curl up like armadillos to protect themselves, and now researchers have developed yet another soft robotic gripper that mimics the tip of an elephant’s trunk. It’s called EleTac and, according to its creators, can actually “feel” the objects it touches.

Continue Reading

Category: Robotics, Engineering

Tags: Japan Advanced Institute of Science and Technology, Biomimicry, Elephant, Touch

items.title: [AI Feed Curator] State of the Feed — Week of July 13–19, 2026
items.content_html:
Six aggregation runs completed during the week of July 13–19, 2026, processing an average of 1077 articles per run with an average yield of 70 quality articles per run. The largest content category was news at 150 articles, followed by ai-tech with 71 articles, wellness with 49 articles, science with 46 articles, local with 44 articles, climate with 39 articles, homelab with 15 articles, and scifi with 9 articles. The dimensional Q/R/L composite scoring model applied weights of 0.25 for quality, 0.55 for relevance, and 0.20 for local signals.

Pipeline efficiency measured a mean noise-to-signal ratio of 2.07 articles fetched per 1 delivered, ranging from 1.23 to 2.76 across the 14-run observation period. This represents typical operational performance within acceptable bounds. Four new feeds were successfully integrated: Tŝilhqot'in National Government, EBSCO, ScienceAlert, and Neuroscience News – ScienceDaily, alongside Hacker News and BC Wildfire Service. No feed errors occurred during the week. Top discovery candidates for potential integration scored as follows: Tŝilhqot'in National Government at 70.0, EBSCO at 69.4, and 10ideas.bearblog.dev at 63.9.

New Feeds This Week

  • Tŝilhqot’in National Government
  • EBSCO
  • ScienceAlert
  • Neuroscience News -- ScienceDaily
  • Hacker News
  • BC Wildfire Service

Weekly Article Distribution

CategoryArticles
local44
ai-tech71
climate39
homelab15
wellness49
science46
scifi9
news150

Discovery Highlights

  • Tŝilhqot’in National Government (score: 70.0)
  • EBSCO (score: 69.4)
  • 10ideas.bearblog.dev (score: 63.9)

Pipeline Efficiency: Noise-to-Signal Ratio

Noise-to-signal = (articles fetched − articles in final feeds) ÷ final total. Lower is more efficient.

WeekMean N:SMinMaxRuns
2026-07-122.091.232.767
2026-07-192.071.232.7614

Quality Review

366 articles across 8 feeds. Scrub pass flagged 13 article(s), 13 recommended for removal.

FeedArticlesAvg compositeStaleTop source
🤖 AI/ML & Tech6539.043TechRadar (20.0%)
🌍 Climate & Energy3546.128Pique Newsmagazine (11.4%)

📰 General News12342.878NYT Top Stories (8.9%)
🔬 Science3851.627ScienceDaily (36.8%)
🚀 Sci-Fi & Culture612.73Reactor Magazine (33.3%)
🌿 Health & Wellness4740.135STAT News (36.2%)

Corpus alignment: 1228 articles analysed — 492 direct-qualify, 130 rescue-dependent, 476 stranded, 36 filler (2.9%). Full report.

Content type breakdown: null: 585, breaking: 278, feature: 125, analysis: 122, fluff: 77, news: 36, opinion: 19, wire: 11, recap: 5, investigation: 1.

User Feedback Audit

667 articles rated (2026-06-17 → 2026-07-19): 222 good, 14 interesting, 431 bad (64.6% bad). Theme-day corrections: 181 of 660 (27.4%). Full audit.

Score bandRated% good% bad

40-5921040.054.8
20-3911732.567.5
0-1916010.088.1

API Cost Review

6 run(s) this week · total est. cost $1.9098 · avg $0.3183/run · 660,316 Claude tokens

VendorCalls (week)Avg/runRateEst. weekly cost
Claude9716.2token-based—
Cohere9616.0$0.0020/call$0.1920
Brave25242.0free$0
Kagi15125.2$0.0075/call$1.1325

Calibration

KnobOldNewRationale
limits.min_claude_score1618User feedback threshold_sweep shows min_claude_score=20 would cut 28.1% of bad articles at 5.7% good lost—well within acceptable bounds. Current value of 16 (raised July 12) cuts 25.3% of bad at 4.1% good lost, an improvement but still insufficient given 63.3% overall bad rate. Proposing 18 as a conservative step toward the 20 ceiling: final feed sizes average 341/run (well above ~100 target), so we have capacity to tighten. This complements the existing haiku_scrub_floor=13 change and directly addresses the user-verified quality issue without risking the feed-scarcity overshoot that triggered June 24's manual intervention. The 18 threshold would cut an estimated 26-27% of bad articles (interpolating between sweep points) while losing ~5% of good articles—a favorable trade given current bad rate.

Actions Taken This Week

items.title: [MacRumors] Apple and DOJ Hold Early Settlement Talks in iPhone Antitrust Case
items.content_html:
🔧 ✏️

🔧 ✏️

🔧 ✏️

🔧 ✏️

🔧 ✏️

Apple is working to settle an antitrust lawsuit with the U.S. Department of Justice, reports Bloomberg. Apple has proposed several offers and is now in early settlement talks with the DOJ.


The DOJ sued Apple for antitrust violations in 2024 as part of an expansive antitrust investigation into major tech companies. The government accused Apple of getting customers "hooked" on its platform through a long-running history of anti-competitive behavior.

The lawsuit accused Apple of restricting "super apps" with broad capabilities, limiting cloud gaming, preferencing its own Messages app over third-party apps, and limiting the function of third-party digital wallets and smartwatches.

Since the lawsuit was filed in 2024, Apple policy changes have addressed many of the claims, weakening the DOJ's position.

While the talks are ongoing, Bloomberg says there's no guarantee an agreement will be reached. Apple attempted to have the lawsuit dismissed last year, but was unsuccessful. So far, no trial has been scheduled.
Tags: Apple Antitrust, Apple vs. DoJ, DOJ, Apple Lawsuits

This article, "Apple and DOJ Hold Early Settlement Talks in iPhone Antitrust Case" first appeared on MacRumors.com

Discuss this article in our forums
